15-1252.what_they_do: Software developers design, create, and test software applications and systems. They analyze user needs, write code, and collaborate with teams to build everything from mobile apps to operating systems.
15-1252.work_environment: Most work in offices or remotely from home. Standard 40-hour weeks are common, though deadlines may require extra hours. Work is primarily at a computer, often in team settings using agile methodologies.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in computer science, software engineering, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions typically do not require prior experience; internships are helpful.
how_to_become.training: Developers continuously learn new languages, frameworks, and tools on the job. Many employers provide training programs.

15-1211.what_they_do: Computer systems analysts study an organization's current computer systems and design solutions to help the organization operate more efficiently. They bridge the gap between IT and business.
15-1211.work_environment: Most work in offices, though remote work is increasingly common. They typically work full time and may travel to client sites.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in computer science, information science, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Some positions require experience in a related IT occupation.
how_to_become.training: On-the-job training to learn the organization's specific systems is common.

15-1212.what_they_do: Information security analysts plan and carry out security measures to protect an organization's computer networks and systems from cyber attacks and data breaches.
15-1212.work_environment: Most work in offices for computer companies or consulting firms. They work full time and may be on call outside normal hours for emergencies.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in cybersecurity, computer science, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Most positions require some experience in a related IT occupation.
how_to_become.training: Security certifications (CompTIA Security+, CISSP) are often required or preferred. Continuous training is essential as threats evolve.

15-2051.what_they_do: Data scientists use analytical tools and techniques to extract meaningful insights from large datasets. They build predictive models, design experiments, and communicate findings to stakeholders.
15-2051.work_environment: Most work in offices for technology, finance, or healthcare companies. Remote work is common. Work is highly collaborative and computer-intensive.

how_to_become.education: Master's degree in data science, statistics, mathematics, or computer science.
how_to_become.experience: Some employers accept candidates with a bachelor's degree and relevant experience.
how_to_become.training: Continuous learning in machine learning frameworks, programming languages (Python, R), and domain-specific knowledge.

29-1141.what_they_do: Registered nurses provide and coordinate patient care, educate patients about health conditions, and provide advice and emotional support to patients and their families.
29-1141.work_environment: Nurses work in hospitals, clinics, nursing homes, and other healthcare facilities. Many work nights, weekends, and holidays. The work can be physically demanding and emotionally challenging.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) or an associate's degree in nursing (ADN). Must pass the NCLEX-RN exam.
how_to_become.experience: Clinical rotations during education provide hands-on experience. No prior work experience required for entry-level positions.
how_to_become.training: New nurses typically complete an orientation program. Specializations (ICU, ER, pediatrics) require additional training.

29-1071.what_they_do: Physician assistants examine patients, diagnose illnesses, prescribe medications, and develop treatment plans under the supervision of physicians and surgeons.
29-1071.work_environment: Work in physicians' offices, hospitals, or outpatient care centers. May work long hours, including evenings and weekends. Physically active — standing and walking for extended periods.

how_to_become.education: Master's degree from an accredited PA program. Must pass the PANCE exam for licensure.
how_to_become.experience: Most PA programs require some healthcare experience (e.g., as an EMT, nurse, or medical assistant).
how_to_become.training: PA programs include classroom instruction and clinical rotations. Must complete continuing education to maintain certification.

29-1021.what_they_do: Dentists diagnose and treat problems with patients' teeth, gums, and related parts of the mouth. They provide advice and instruction on oral hygiene and preventive care.
29-1021.work_environment: Most dentists own their own practices. They work in clean, well-lit offices. Some work evenings and weekends to accommodate patients' schedules.

how_to_become.education: Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) or Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD) from an accredited dental school (4 years after bachelor's).
how_to_become.experience: Clinical experience is gained during dental school. No prior work experience is required.
how_to_become.training: All states require dentists to be licensed. Specializations require 2-6 years of additional residency training.

29-1051.what_they_do: Pharmacists dispense prescription medications and advise patients on proper use, side effects, and interactions. They also administer vaccinations and provide health screenings.
29-1051.work_environment: Work in pharmacies (retail, hospital, or clinical settings). Many work full time, including evenings, weekends, and holidays. Must stand for long periods.

how_to_become.education: Doctor of Pharmacy (Pharm.D.) degree — a 4-year professional program after at least 2 years of undergraduate study.
how_to_become.experience: No prior work experience required, but pharmacy internships during the Pharm.D. program are mandatory.
how_to_become.training: Must pass the NAPLEX and MPJE exams for licensure. Some pharmacists complete 1-2 year residency programs.

13-2011.what_they_do: Accountants prepare and examine financial records, ensure taxes are paid properly and on time, and assess financial operations to help organizations run efficiently.
13-2011.work_environment: Most work in offices. During tax season or end-of-budget cycles, they may work long hours. Some accountants are self-employed or work for accounting firms.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in accounting or a related field. CPA certification requires 150 credit hours (more than a typical bachelor's).
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions generally do not require experience. CPA certification and master's degrees improve advancement opportunities.
how_to_become.training: Most states require CPAs to complete continuing professional education (CPE) to maintain their licenses.

13-2051.what_they_do: Financial analysts guide businesses and individuals in making investment decisions. They assess the performance of stocks, bonds, and other investments.
13-2051.work_environment: Most work in offices at banks, insurance companies, or securities firms. They typically work full time and may work more than 40 hours per week.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in finance, economics, accounting, or statistics.
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions may be available without experience. Senior positions may require CFA certification.
how_to_become.training: Financial analysts may need to obtain licenses such as the Series 7 or Series 63. Continuing education is important.

11-2021.what_they_do: Marketing managers plan programs to generate interest in products or services. They work with advertising, promotions, and sales teams to develop pricing strategies and campaigns.
11-2021.work_environment: Most work in offices. They may travel to meet clients or attend conferences. Work can be stressful due to tight deadlines and high expectations.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in marketing, business administration, or communications.
how_to_become.experience: Typically requires several years of experience in advertising, marketing, or sales. Often promoted from lower-level marketing positions.
how_to_become.training: On-the-job training in digital marketing tools, analytics platforms, and industry-specific knowledge.

11-3031.what_they_do: Financial managers create financial reports, direct investment activities, and develop plans for the long-term financial goals of their organization.
11-3031.work_environment: Work in offices at banks, insurance companies, or corporate headquarters. Most work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, economics, or business administration.
how_to_become.experience: Typically requires 5+ years of experience in a related business or financial occupation.
how_to_become.training: Professional certifications such as CFA or CPA can be beneficial. Many employers provide management training programs.

17-2051.what_they_do: Civil engineers design, build, and supervise infrastructure projects such as roads, bridges, buildings, and water systems.
17-2051.work_environment: Civil engineers split time between offices and construction sites. They typically work full time and may work overtime to meet project deadlines.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in civil engineering or civil engineering technology from an ABET-accredited program.
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions require a bachelor's degree. Professional Engineer (PE) license requires 4+ years of experience.
how_to_become.training: Must pass the FE exam after graduation and PE exam after gaining experience. Continuing education is required for license renewal.

17-2071.what_they_do: Electrical engineers design, develop, test, and supervise the manufacture of electrical equipment, from power generation to consumer electronics.
17-2071.work_environment: Work in offices, labs, and manufacturing plants. Most work full time and may need to travel to oversee installations or production.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in electrical engineering or electrical engineering technology.
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions typically require a bachelor's degree only. Advanced positions may require PE licensure.
how_to_become.training: Engineers must keep up with rapidly advancing technology through continuing education and professional development.

17-2141.what_they_do: Mechanical engineers design, develop, and test mechanical and thermal devices, including tools, engines, and machines.
17-2141.work_environment: Work in offices, labs, or industrial manufacturing plants. May visit work sites to troubleshoot equipment problems.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or mechanical engineering technology.
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions require a bachelor's degree. Co-op programs and internships during college are valuable.
how_to_become.training: FE and PE exams are required for licensure. Many engineers earn certifications in specialized areas such as robotics or HVAC.

25-2021.what_they_do: Elementary school teachers instruct young students in basic subjects such as math, reading, science, and social studies, using various teaching methods to engage children in learning.
25-2021.work_environment: Work in public and private schools. Follow a 10-month school year schedule with summers off. May spend evenings and weekends grading and preparing lessons.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in elementary education or a content area. Must complete a teacher preparation program.
how_to_become.experience: Student teaching experience is required during education. No prior work experience needed for entry-level positions.
how_to_become.training: Must be licensed/certified by the state. Teachers must complete continuing education and professional development hours.

25-2031.what_they_do: High school teachers teach academic lessons and skills to students in grades 9 through 12. They specialize in a subject such as English, math, science, or history.
25-2031.work_environment: Work in public and private high schools. Follow the academic school year calendar. Extracurricular responsibilities (coaching, clubs) may extend hours.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree, usually in education or the subject they teach. Must complete a teacher preparation program.
how_to_become.experience: Student teaching experience is required. Some states accept alternative certification routes for career changers.
how_to_become.training: State licensure/certification required. Ongoing professional development and recertification requirements.

49-9021.what_they_do: HVAC technicians install, maintain, and repair heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration systems in homes, offices, and commercial buildings.
49-9021.work_environment: Work indoors and outdoors, sometimes in uncomfortable conditions (extreme heat/cold, tight spaces). May work evenings/weekends for emergency repairs.

how_to_become.education: Postsecondary certificate or associate's degree from a trade school or community college HVAC program.
how_to_become.experience: Many start as apprentices, learning on the job while earning wages.
how_to_become.training: EPA Section 608 certification required to handle refrigerants. Many states require licensure. Apprenticeships typically last 3-5 years.

47-2111.what_they_do: Electricians install, maintain, and repair electrical power, communications, lighting, and control systems in homes, businesses, and factories.
47-2111.work_environment: Work indoors and outdoors at construction sites, homes, and businesses. Physical work involving climbing, kneeling, and lifting. Risk of electrical shock and falls.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma or equivalent. Many complete a postsecondary certificate program at a trade school.
how_to_become.experience: Most electricians learn through a 4-5 year apprenticeship, combining paid on-the-job training with classroom instruction.
how_to_become.training: Most states require electricians to be licensed. Must pass competency exams. Continuing education required for license renewal.

29-2061.what_they_do: Licensed practical nurses (LPNs) provide basic nursing care under the direction of registered nurses and doctors, including taking vital signs, administering medications, and assisting patients with daily activities.
29-2061.work_environment: Work in nursing homes, hospitals, physicians' offices, and home healthcare. May work nights, weekends, and holidays. Physically demanding work.

how_to_become.education: State-approved certificate or diploma program in practical nursing (about 1 year). Must pass the NCLEX-PN exam.
how_to_become.experience: No prior work experience required. Clinical practice during the education program provides hands-on experience.
how_to_become.training: On-the-job orientation is typical. LPNs can pursue further education to become registered nurses.

15-1231.what_they_do: Web developers design and create websites. They handle the site's technical aspects, such as performance, capacity, and functionality, as well as its visual appearance.
15-1231.work_environment: Most work in offices or remotely. Freelancing is common. Work is primarily at a computer, often with tight deadlines.

how_to_become.education: Associate's degree in web design, computer science, or related field. Some positions accept candidates with a certificate or self-taught portfolio.
how_to_become.experience: A strong portfolio of web projects can substitute for formal experience. Internships are valuable.
how_to_become.training: Constant self-learning of new frameworks, libraries, and web standards is essential. Bootcamps and online courses are popular training paths.

23-1011.what_they_do: Lawyers advise and represent individuals, businesses, and government agencies on legal issues and disputes. They research laws, prepare legal documents, and argue cases in court.
23-1011.work_environment: Most work in law firms, corporate legal departments, or government agencies. Long hours are common, especially in private practice. High-pressure environment.

how_to_become.education: Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree from a law school accredited by the ABA (3 years after bachelor's). Must pass the bar exam.
how_to_become.experience: Law school internships and clerkships provide experience. Many firms offer summer associate programs.
how_to_become.training: Must pass the bar exam in the state(s) where they wish to practice. Continuing legal education (CLE) is required.

23-2011.what_they_do: Paralegals assist lawyers by organizing files, drafting documents, researching legal precedents, and preparing for trials. They cannot give legal advice or represent clients in court.
23-2011.work_environment: Most work in law firms. Some work for corporate legal departments or government agencies. Office work with occasional overtime during trial preparation.

how_to_become.education: Associate's degree in paralegal studies or a bachelor's degree with a paralegal certificate.
how_to_become.experience: Entry-level positions are available without prior experience. Internships during education are helpful.
how_to_become.training: On-the-job training is common. Voluntary certification (such as CLA or CP) can improve job prospects.

27-1024.what_they_do: Graphic designers create visual concepts using computer software or by hand to communicate ideas that inspire, inform, and captivate consumers through logos, layouts, and images.
27-1024.work_environment: Work in specialized design firms, advertising agencies, or as freelancers. Studio or office settings. Deadlines can create pressure.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in graphic design, fine arts, or a related field. A strong portfolio is essential.
how_to_become.experience: Internships and freelance projects during education help build a portfolio. Entry-level positions are available for graduates with strong portfolios.
how_to_become.training: Designers must stay current with design software (Adobe Creative Suite), UX/UI trends, and emerging technologies.

27-3023.what_they_do: News analysts and reporters research, write, and report news stories for newspapers, magazines, websites, television, and radio. They gather information through interviews, investigations, and observation.
27-3023.work_environment: Work in newsrooms, studios, or in the field. Schedules are often irregular — breaking news doesn't follow a 9-to-5. May travel extensively.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in journalism, communications, English, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Internships at newspapers, TV stations, or digital media outlets are highly valuable. School newspaper experience helps.
how_to_become.training: On-the-job training under experienced reporters. Learning multimedia skills (video editing, social media) is increasingly important.

17-1011.what_they_do: Architects plan and design buildings, homes, and other structures. They meet with clients, create blueprints, and ensure designs meet safety codes while balancing aesthetics and function.
17-1011.work_environment: Work in offices, though they frequently visit construction sites. Most work full time. Deadlines may require overtime, especially as projects near completion.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor of Architecture (B.Arch, 5-year program) or a master's degree in architecture.
how_to_become.experience: Most states require a paid internship (Architectural Experience Program) before licensing. The ARE exam is required for licensure.
how_to_become.training: Continuous learning in sustainable design, building codes, and new CAD/BIM software tools.

17-2031.what_they_do: Biomedical engineers combine engineering principles with medical and biological sciences to design healthcare solutions — prosthetics, medical devices, imaging equipment, and biocompatible materials.
17-2031.work_environment: Work in hospitals, laboratories, research facilities, or manufacturing plants. Standard full-time hours with occasional overtime for project deadlines.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in biomedical engineering or a related engineering field.
how_to_become.experience: Internships and co-op programs in medical device companies are valuable. Entry-level positions available with a bachelor's degree.
how_to_become.training: On-the-job training in FDA regulations, quality systems, and specific medical technologies.

27-1014.what_they_do: Multimedia artists and animators create visual effects, animation, and other visual images using computers and creative tools for movies, video games, television, and other media.
27-1014.work_environment: Work in offices or studios for film, gaming, or advertising companies. Freelancing is common. Deadlines often require long hours, especially near project milestones.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in fine arts, computer graphics, animation, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: A strong portfolio or demo reel is essential. Internships at studios or personal projects help build a body of work.
how_to_become.training: Continuous learning of new animation software (Maya, Blender, After Effects) and emerging techniques like motion capture.

21-1021.what_they_do: Social workers help people solve and cope with problems in their everyday lives. They may assist with issues like substance abuse, child welfare, mental health, poverty, and family conflicts.
21-1021.work_environment: Work in schools, hospitals, mental health clinics, and government agencies. May travel to visit clients. Emotionally demanding work with irregular hours.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in social work (BSW) for entry-level; master's (MSW) required for clinical positions.
how_to_become.experience: Supervised fieldwork during degree programs provides hands-on experience.
how_to_become.training: State licensure required. Continuing education to maintain licensure and stay current with best practices.

21-1018.what_they_do: Mental health counselors help individuals dealing with anxiety, depression, addiction, and other behavioral disorders. They develop treatment plans, provide therapy, and connect clients with support resources.
21-1018.work_environment: Work in outpatient care centers, residential treatment facilities, hospitals, or private practice. May have evening/weekend hours to accommodate clients.

how_to_become.education: Master's degree in counseling, psychology, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Supervised clinical experience (2,000-4,000 hours) is required before independent licensure.
how_to_become.training: State licensure required. Continuing education in evidence-based therapies (CBT, DBT) and ethical practice.

15-1242.what_they_do: Database administrators and architects design, implement, and maintain database systems that organizations use to store and organize data. They ensure data is available, secure, and performing efficiently.
15-1242.work_environment: Work in offices for companies across all industries. Most work full time. Some may be on call for database emergencies outside normal hours.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in computer science, information technology, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Some positions require experience as a database developer or systems administrator.
how_to_become.training: Database certifications (Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, AWS) are valuable. Continuous learning in cloud databases and data security.

47-2152.what_they_do: Plumbers install and repair piping systems in homes, businesses, and factories. They work with water supply lines, drainage systems, gas lines, and related fixtures and appliances.
47-2152.work_environment: Work in homes, commercial buildings, and construction sites. Physically demanding — crawling, lifting, and working in tight spaces. May be on call for emergencies.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma, then a 4-5 year apprenticeship combining on-the-job training with classroom instruction.
how_to_become.experience: Apprenticeships provide paid hands-on training. Must pass a licensing exam in most states.
how_to_become.training: Journeyman and master plumber certifications available. Must stay current with building codes.

47-2031.what_they_do: Carpenters build, install, and repair structures made of wood and other materials. They work on everything from kitchen cabinets and door frames to entire building frameworks.
47-2031.work_environment: Work on construction sites, both indoors and outdoors. Physically demanding — lifting, standing, climbing. May travel to different job sites.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma. Most learn through a 3-4 year apprenticeship program.
how_to_become.experience: Apprenticeships combine paid on-the-job training with technical instruction. Some start as helpers.
how_to_become.training: Specialized certifications available in areas like finish carpentry, scaffolding, and green building.

35-1011.what_they_do: Chefs and head cooks oversee food preparation in restaurants, hotels, and other dining establishments. They create menus, direct kitchen staff, and ensure food quality and safety.
35-1011.work_environment: Work in hot, fast-paced kitchens. Often work early mornings, late evenings, weekends, and holidays. Physically demanding — standing for long hours.

how_to_become.education: Certificate or associate's degree from a culinary school, though many learn through work experience.
how_to_become.experience: Most chefs start as line cooks and work their way up. Years of kitchen experience are valued more than formal education.
how_to_become.training: ServSafe certification required. Specialized training in pastry, cuisine types, or restaurant management.

29-1123.what_they_do: Physical therapists help injured or ill people improve movement and manage pain. They develop treatment plans, guide patients through exercises, and educate them on preventing further injury.
29-1123.work_environment: Work in hospitals, clinics, nursing homes, or patients' homes. Physically active — helping patients move and exercise. Most work full time; some work evenings/weekends.

how_to_become.education: Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) degree — a 3-year program after a bachelor's degree.
how_to_become.experience: Clinical rotations during DPT program. Must pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) for licensure.
how_to_become.training: Board certification available in specialties like sports, geriatrics, or pediatric physical therapy.

29-1131.what_they_do: Veterinarians diagnose and treat diseases and injuries in animals. They perform surgeries, prescribe medications, vaccinate animals, and advise owners on animal care and nutrition.
29-1131.work_environment: Work in veterinary clinics, hospitals, farms, or laboratories. May be on call for emergencies. Some work with large animals and travel to farms.

how_to_become.education: Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree — a 4-year program after completing pre-vet undergraduate coursework.
how_to_become.experience: Veterinary experience (volunteering, technician work) strengthens applications. Must pass the NAVLE for licensure.
how_to_become.training: Residency programs available for specialization (surgery, oncology, cardiology). Board certification through specialty colleges.

29-1292.what_they_do: Dental hygienists clean teeth, examine patients for oral diseases, take dental X-rays, and educate patients on proper oral hygiene. They work closely with dentists.
29-1292.work_environment: Work in dentists' offices in clean clinical settings. Many work part time or flexible schedules. Physical — standing and bending over patients.

how_to_become.education: Associate's degree in dental hygiene from an accredited program.
how_to_become.experience: Clinical training is part of the degree program. Must pass the National Board Dental Hygiene Examination.
how_to_become.training: State licensure required. Continuing education to maintain licensure.

29-1228.what_they_do: Physicians and surgeons diagnose and treat injuries and illnesses. They examine patients, order and interpret tests, prescribe medications, and perform surgeries when necessary.
29-1228.work_environment: Work in hospitals, clinics, and private practices. Long and irregular hours are common, including overnight shifts. Emotionally and physically demanding.

how_to_become.education: Doctor of Medicine (M.D.) or Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine (D.O.) — 4 years of medical school after a bachelor's degree.
how_to_become.experience: 3-7 years of residency training after medical school, depending on specialty. Must pass USMLE or COMLEX for licensure.
how_to_become.training: Board certification in a chosen specialty. Continuing medical education (CME) required to maintain licensure.

29-1122.what_they_do: Occupational therapists help patients develop, recover, and improve the skills needed for daily living and working. They work with people recovering from injuries, disabilities, or illness.
29-1122.work_environment: Work in hospitals, rehabilitation centers, schools, or patients' homes. Physically active. Most work full time; some evening/weekend hours.

how_to_become.education: Master's degree in occupational therapy from an accredited program.
how_to_become.experience: Supervised fieldwork is required during the degree program. Must pass the NBCOT exam for certification.
how_to_become.training: State licensure required. Continuing education to maintain certification. Specializations available.

29-2052.what_they_do: Pharmacy technicians assist pharmacists in dispensing prescription medications. They receive prescription requests, count tablets, label bottles, and process insurance claims.
29-2052.work_environment: Work in pharmacies (retail, hospital, mail-order). Standing for long periods. May work evenings, weekends, and holidays.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma. Many complete postsecondary certificate programs in pharmacy technology.
how_to_become.experience: On-the-job training is common. Certification (PTCB or ExCPT) is required or preferred in many states.
how_to_become.training: Continuing education to maintain certification. Some pursue advanced certifications.

11-9111.what_they_do: Medical and health services managers plan, direct, and coordinate the business activities of healthcare providers. They manage facilities, departments, or medical practices.
11-9111.work_environment: Work in hospitals, physician offices, nursing homes, or group medical practices. Standard office hours, though some facilities operate 24/7.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in health administration, public health, or business administration. Master's preferred for some positions.
how_to_become.experience: Many positions require administrative experience in a healthcare setting.
how_to_become.training: Professional certifications (FACHE, CMPE) are available. Continuous learning in healthcare regulations and technology.

39-9031.what_they_do: Fitness trainers and instructors lead, instruct, and motivate individuals or groups in exercise activities. They design workout programs, demonstrate techniques, and monitor clients' progress.
39-9031.work_environment: Work in gyms, health clubs, recreation centers, or clients' homes. Physically active. Hours vary — early mornings, evenings, and weekends are common.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma required; many have a certificate or associate's degree in fitness or exercise science.
how_to_become.experience: Personal training experience and a strong fitness background are valued.
how_to_become.training: Certification from a nationally recognized organization (ACE, NASM, ACSM) is typically required. CPR/AED certification required.

19-3031.what_they_do: Psychologists study cognitive, emotional, and social processes and behavior. They help people understand and manage mental health conditions, conduct research, and may provide therapy.
19-3031.work_environment: Work in hospitals, schools, mental health centers, research institutions, or private practice. Standard office hours; some offer evening sessions.

how_to_become.education: Doctoral degree (Ph.D. or Psy.D.) in psychology for clinical practice. Some research and industrial positions accept a master's degree.
how_to_become.experience: Supervised clinical experience (internship/postdoctoral) required for licensure.
how_to_become.training: State licensure required for clinical practice. Continuing education to maintain licensure.

19-2041.what_they_do: Environmental scientists study the environment and develop solutions to environmental problems like pollution, climate change, and natural resource management.
19-2041.work_environment: Split between offices, laboratories, and fieldwork outdoors. May travel to study sites. Most work full time; fieldwork may require irregular hours.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in environmental science, biology, chemistry, or a related natural science.
how_to_become.experience: Internships with government agencies or environmental consulting firms are helpful.
how_to_become.training: Professional certifications available. GIS and data analysis skills increasingly important.

19-4092.what_they_do: Forensic science technicians collect and analyze physical evidence from crime scenes. They document scenes, perform lab tests on evidence, and provide expert testimony in court.
19-4092.work_environment: Work in laboratories and at crime scenes. Crime scene work can be outdoors and physically demanding. May work irregular hours responding to crime scenes.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in forensic science, chemistry, biology, or a related natural science.
how_to_become.experience: Laboratory experience and internships with law enforcement agencies are valuable.
how_to_become.training: Specialized training in areas like DNA analysis, ballistics, or digital forensics. Professional certification available.

11-3121.what_they_do: Human resources managers plan, coordinate, and direct the administrative functions of an organization. They oversee recruiting, interviewing, hiring, employee relations, and benefits.
11-3121.work_environment: Work in offices across nearly every industry. Standard full-time hours. Some travel for recruiting events or to visit branch offices.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in human resources, business administration, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Several years of experience as an HR specialist or generalist before advancing to management.
how_to_become.training: Professional certifications (PHR, SPHR, SHRM-CP) are valued. Staying current with employment law is essential.

27-3031.what_they_do: Public relations specialists create and maintain a favorable public image for the organizations they represent. They write press releases, manage media relations, and develop communication strategies.
27-3031.work_environment: Work in offices, though some travel for events or media appearances. Fast-paced with tight deadlines. Some crisis management requires after-hours work.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree in public relations, communications, journalism, or a related field.
how_to_become.experience: Internships in PR agencies or communications departments are valuable. Entry-level positions are available.
how_to_become.training: Accreditation in Public Relations (APR) available. Social media and digital marketing skills are increasingly important.

33-3051.what_they_do: Police officers protect lives and property by enforcing laws, responding to emergencies, patrolling communities, conducting investigations, and apprehending suspects.
33-3051.work_environment: Work in patrol cars, on foot, and in offices. Shift work including nights, weekends, and holidays. Physically demanding and potentially dangerous.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma required; many agencies prefer some college coursework or an associate's/bachelor's degree in criminal justice.
how_to_become.experience: Must complete a police academy training program (12-14 weeks). Background check and physical fitness test required.
how_to_become.training: Field training after the academy. Continuing education in law, tactics, and community policing.

33-2011.what_they_do: Firefighters respond to fires and other emergencies. They extinguish fires, provide emergency medical services, perform rescue operations, and educate the public about fire safety.
33-2011.work_environment: Work at fire stations with 24-hour shifts (e.g., 24 on / 48 off). Physically demanding and dangerous. Must maintain peak physical fitness.

how_to_become.education: High school diploma required. Many complete fire science certificate or associate's degree programs.
how_to_become.experience: Must complete fire academy training. EMT certification is typically required.
how_to_become.training: Ongoing training in firefighting techniques, hazmat handling, and emergency medical procedures.

53-2011.what_they_do: Airline pilots fly and navigate airplanes for airlines, transporting passengers and cargo. They perform pre-flight inspections, communicate with air traffic control, and ensure safe takeoffs and landings.
53-2011.work_environment: Work in cockpits. Away from home frequently, with overnight layovers. Irregular schedules including nights, weekends, and holidays. FAA limits flight hours.

how_to_become.education: Bachelor's degree (any field accepted by airlines, though aviation-related degrees are common). FAA Airline Transport Pilot (ATP) certificate required.
how_to_become.experience: 1,500 flight hours minimum for an ATP certificate. Many start as flight instructors or regional airline pilots.
how_to_become.training: Type ratings for specific aircraft. Regular proficiency checks and medical exams required by the FAA.
